author: GamesIndustry.biz
text:
Stellar Blade studio Shift Up breaks from publishing partner Sony for "high-quality self-publishing"

South Korean developer Shift Up has outlined plans to self-publish Stellar Blade 2.

author: GamesIndustry.biz
text:
Nintendo share value falls as president admits increased prices will "raise the barrier to purchase"

Nintendo shares have dropped after president Shuntaro Furukawa acknowledged that raising the prices on its hardware "will raise the barrier to purchase."

Read more

author: GamesIndustry.biz
text:
Sony maps out how first-party PlayStation studios are utilising AI tools during development

Sony has detailed its plans to expand AI integration throughout its organisation, including game development.

Read more

author: GamesIndustry.biz
text:
Why the founder of Kongregate believes that Roblox is its successor

User-generated content (UGC) in the gaming space is a buzzy topic, with platforms like Roblox, Fortnite, Minecraft and even GTA Online serving as a platform for thousands of creators to build their own games and in some cases their own fortunes – but it's not a new one.

Read more

author: GamesIndustry.biz
text:
Guerrilla Games co-founder Arjan Brussee announces plans to build European-based games engine

Guerrilla Games co-founder Arjan Brussee has announced plans to develop a game engine positioned as "a European alternative" to competitors such as Unreal and Unity.

Read more

author: GamesIndustry.biz
text:
Double Fine Productions is the latest Microsoft subsidiary to unionise

Psychonauts developer Double Fine Productions has filed to unionise with support from the Communications Workers of America (CWA).

Read more

author: GamesIndustry.biz
text:
Industry veteran and YouTube musician launch new indie studio, Stupendium Softworks

Industry veteran Alex Oxspring and YouTube musician The Stupendium have established the independent studio Stupendium Softworks.

Read more

author: GamesIndustry.biz
text:
Atari acquires rights to first five Wizardry RPGs

Atari has acquired the "complete and exclusive rights" to the first five games in the original Wizardry RPG series, including related intellectual property from the original rights holder.

Read more
